title: Dalea lloydii
taxonomy: Dalea lloydii is a member of the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that also includes beans, peas, and clover. Dalea lloydii is further classified into the subfamily Faboideae, which is the largest subfamily within the Fabaceae family. It belongs to the tribe Amorpheae, and its genus Dalea is a group of shrubs and small trees native to North America. The classification of Dalea lloydii reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Fabaceae family.

intro: Dalea lloydii, also known as Lloyd's spineflower, is a flowering plant species in the pea family (Fabaceae) that is native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and wounds, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Dalea lloydii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Dalea lloydii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and to support immune function.
